project_name: Developing Inclusive Education in the Mediterranean
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: Sapienza University of Rome
topic_keywords: training | trainings | learning | education | course | skills | capacity building | capacity development | capacity | knowledge transfer | toolkit | dissemination | communication | replication | scalability | beneficiaries | youth | women | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean is united by some major challenges related to the development of its different areas and communities, especially regarding the gap between cultural heritage developmental potential and the social and economic precariousness prevailing around important cultural sites.
Education, continuous training and skills development represent a key tool both for strengthening the potential of territories and for promoting inclusion, empowerment and social cohesion.
Furthermore, Cultural Heritage (CH) is one of the most sound, common and recognizable Mediterranean traits as well as an acknowledged driver for its development.
Accordingly, its main objective will be reinforcing and innovating the consortium's different learning/teaching systems to make them more integrated and compatible. Besides, the focus on Cultural Heritage will allow DIEM to exploit the acknowledged multiplier effect of CH-oriented activities in terms of know-how exchanging, empowerment of people and, eventually, economic growth.
Hence, DIEM is expected to contribute to social inclusion and to skills development for the unintegrated groups (youth, women, rural inhabitants and NEETs). Capitalizing on the experience of its partners, DIEM will move along 3 concurrent lines of development: deliver a cascade model of teaching/training programs for 3 groups (A-Leaders, B-Future Managers, C-Local communities); setup a toolkit for teaching/learning capable to combine knowledge, emerging technologies (i.e. AI) and the lessons learned from the pandemic; assume DIEM as an occasion for the first beneficiaries and for broader communities, the assets and territories belonging to the 6 countries of DIEM consortium (IT, EGY, JOR, TUN, ES,PS).
The project entails 3 technical WPs: Standardization (Partners design systems for international cooperation), Training around pilot areas (TOT courses and inclusion-oriented trainings), Scalability (replication on youth and extension to broader communities).
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Cultural heritage and arts, Education and training, Social inclusion and equal opportunities

project_name: Social Ecological Impact Assessment for the Maritime Sector in the Atlantic Area
partner_name: La Palma Research Centre (La Palma Office)
topic_keywords: methodology | methodologies | toolkit | knowledge exchange | uptake | sme | smes | platform
project_summary:
Impact4Mar aims to accelerate the transition of SMEs in the maritime sector of the Atlantic area by facilitating an innovative access to tools and methodologies for assessing Impact, Risks and Opportunities (IRO) in Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) dimensions.
Through transnational knowledge exchange, Impact4Mar proposes an integrated approach based on a common methodology across the regional maritime ecosystems involved to: - Conduct an analysis of sustainability IROs assessment practices in the maritime sector - Prototype and validate a Toolkit through pilots in maritime SMEs -Capitalize and disseminate the results through a digital platform and bootcamps.
By offering maritime SME-focused solutions, the project empowers businesses to comply with EU sustainability regulations, align with the EU Green Deal and UN 2030 Agenda and enhance resilience, competitiveness and innovation as a catalyst for the Blue Economy’s sustainable development in Europe.

partner_name: Università degli Studi di Bari Aldo Moro

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

028. Technology transfer and cooperation between enterprises, research centres and higher education sector
~
SUMMARY: The HERACLES Project aims to establish and strengthen a multi-actor research network between companies and universities, focusing on the application of AI to enhance predictive medicine and health crisis preparedness, addressing significant common challenges in the Mediterranean region and transforming healthcare, making it more efficient. The project promotes Open Innovation and Open Research paradigms, exploring AI's potential alongside legal and regulatory frameworks to ensure proper valorisation of research through intellectual property and technology transfer mechanisms. Key activities of the HERACLES Project include implementing a Pilot Project that develops collaboration schemes between stakeholders at a transnational level, facilitating the exchange of researchers and entrepreneurs. These schemes aim to foster interaction between the scientific research community and the health technology business sector, integrating various stakeholders to develop AI-based tools specialised in predictive medicine and crisis management. Additionally, the project will explore and develop innovative business models to transform scientific research results into tangible commercial opportunities. By addressing common healthcare challenges through a collaborative and innovative approach, the HERACLES Project promises substantial benefits. It aims to drive forward a new era of AI-enhanced medical care, ultimately improving healthcare systems across the Mediterranean region and ensuring a more robust response to health crises. The expected changes include improved patient outcomes, more efficient allocation of healthcare resources, and the integration of advanced AI technologies into everyday clinical practice, benefiting patients, healthcare providers, and the broader medical community.
~

project_name: EmpoweerNEET MED: Upskilling NEETs through Social and Solidarity Economy
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: Associació Fons Català de Cooperació al Desenvolupament
topic_keywords: training | learning | education | workshop | skills | upskilling | capacity | guidelines | handbook | dissemination | communication | entrepreneur | entrepreneurs | entrepreneurship | startup | platform | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: EmpowerNEET is an innovative transnational initiative aimed at integrating NEETs—young people not engaged in employment, education, or training—into the Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) across the Mediterranean. These individuals face significant challenges, including high unemployment rates, economic exclusion, and limited access to quality education and vocational training, exacerbating social inequalities. EmpowerNEET leverages the underutilised potential of the SSE to promote inclusive economic growth and social integration.

By facilitating the transnational sharing of best practices and expertise, EmpowerNEET brings together entities from Palestine, Spain, Greece, Tunisia, Lebanon, and France to effectively address NEET marginalisation. The project offers comprehensive training programs to develop entrepreneurial and employment skills, provides practical experiences through job shadowing and mentoring, and ensures sustainability via supportive policy frameworks and ethical financing models.

EmpowerNEET aims to transition NEETs from economic and social marginalisation to empowerment. Key outputs include an extensive capacity-building program that equips 300 NEETs with essential theoretical and practical skills tailored for the SSE. Additionally, the project collaborates with local authorities to develop sustainable financing strategies, securing the long-term success of SSE initiatives and providing systemic support for NEETs.

The expected change includes improved employability and entrepreneurial capacity among NEETs. This project will benefit NEETs by enhancing their skills and opportunities, local governments by providing robust frameworks for NEETs and SSE support, and the broader community by stimulating economic growth and social cohesion. EmpowerNEET not only enhances the economic prospects of NEETs but also strengthens local communities by supporting SSEs, boosting economic vitality, and fostering inclusive growth.
~

project_name: Sustainable Microalgae and Cyanobacteria-Based Bioactive Compounds for Cosmeceuticals and Nutraceuticals
partner_name: Aqualgae Sociedade Limitada (Research and Development)
topic_keywords: knowledge transfer | pilot action | pilot actions | uptake | scalable | sme | smes
project_summary:
A pioneering initiative set to transform the cosmeceutical and nutraceutical industries by utilizing bioactives and biopolymers from microalgae. This project introduces innovative, low-impact methods to produce high-value ingredients, replacing synthetic additives while reducing water use and minimizing waste.
By optimizing microalgae cultivation and recovering valuable by-products, the project demonstrates scalable solutions that support a shift toward renewable, eco-friendly alternatives. Key outputs include pilot actions, innovative biotechnological solutions, and high-value bio-based products for industrial adoption, benefiting SMEs, researchers, and blue biotechnology industries.
With a transnational approach, the initiative integrates regional expertise, fosters knowledge transfer, and drives sustainable growth in the Atlantic’s blue economy. By accelerating innovation and competitiveness in the bioeconomy sector,it paves the way for a more sustainable future.

Impact4Mar aims to accelerate the transition of SMEs in the maritime sector of the Atlantic area by facilitating an innovative access to tools and methodologies for assessing Impact, Risks and Opportunities (IRO) in Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) dimensions.
Through transnational knowledge exchange, Impact4Mar proposes an integrated approach based on a common methodology across the regional maritime ecosystems involved to: - Conduct an analysis of sustainability IROs assessment practices in the maritime sector - Prototype and validate a Toolkit through pilots in maritime SMEs -Capitalize and disseminate the results through a digital platform and bootcamps.
By offering maritime SME-focused solutions, the project empowers businesses to comply with EU sustainability regulations, align with the EU Green Deal and UN 2030 Agenda and enhance resilience, competitiveness and innovation as a catalyst for the Blue Economy’s sustainable development in Europe.

PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

028. Technology transfer and cooperation between enterprises, research centres and higher education sector
~
SUMMARY: The HERACLES Project aims to establish and strengthen a multi-actor research network between companies and universities, focusing on the application of AI to enhance predictive medicine and health crisis preparedness, addressing significant common challenges in the Mediterranean region and transforming healthcare, making it more efficient. The project promotes Open Innovation and Open Research paradigms, exploring AI's potential alongside legal and regulatory frameworks to ensure proper valorisation of research through intellectual property and technology transfer mechanisms. Key activities of the HERACLES Project include implementing a Pilot Project that develops collaboration schemes between stakeholders at a transnational level, facilitating the exchange of researchers and entrepreneurs. These schemes aim to foster interaction between the scientific research community and the health technology business sector, integrating various stakeholders to develop AI-based tools specialised in predictive medicine and crisis management. Additionally, the project will explore and develop innovative business models to transform scientific research results into tangible commercial opportunities. By addressing common healthcare challenges through a collaborative and innovative approach, the HERACLES Project promises substantial benefits. It aims to drive forward a new era of AI-enhanced medical care, ultimately improving healthcare systems across the Mediterranean region and ensuring a more robust response to health crises. The expected changes include improved patient outcomes, more efficient allocation of healthcare resources, and the integration of advanced AI technologies into everyday clinical practice, benefiting patients, healthcare providers, and the broader medical community.
~

project_name: Mediterranean initiative to REgenerate wastewater Sludges for Optimal Use towaRd a Circular Economy
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: Università degli Studi di Cagliari
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | toolkit | guidelines | communication | reuse | beneficiaries | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

066. Waste water collection and treatment compliant with energy efficiency criteria
~
SUMMARY: MedRESOURCE tackles the pressing transnational challenges of water scarcity, land degradation, and waste management in the Mediterranean region. Currently, despite existing standards, WW sludge is still inadequately managed, leading to environmental harm and missed opportunities for resource recovery. MedRESOURCE will develop and demonstrate four innovative solutions to reuse wastewater sludge to enhance soil fertility, reduce environmental pollution, and foster sustainable agricultural practices.
The solutions will be jointly developed by a partnership of 6 organizations from 4 countries: Palestine, Italy, Tunisia and Jordan, supported by institutional and local associated partners. In each country a Living Lab will be set up to further develop existing solutions and to demonstrate their value to local communities, farmers, authorities and policy makers.
The expected change from MedRESOURCE is profound: converting waste into valuable resources, the project will significantly improve soil quality, reduce the need for chemical fertilizers, and mitigate pollution. This will enhance agricultural productivity and resilience to climate change, benefiting local farmers, communities, and the environment.
Key outputs are:
-	4 Living Labs (LL), each demonstrating a specific reuse of WW sludge in agriculture
-	A capacity building programme carried out in each LL, reaching about 200 beneficiaries
-	4 international study visist to the LL, promoting replicability and best practice exchange and reaching from 60 to 120 beneficiaries
-	Guidelines for policies development, providing references and standards at EU and MED level to integrate and better adapt regulatory frameworks to the innovative potential of WW sludge reuse.
MedRESOURCE offers a compelling solution to critical environmental issues, providing tangible benefits and fostering sustainable development across the Mediterranean. Supporting this project means investing in a greener, more resilient future for the region.
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Soil and air quality, Sustainable management of natural resources, Water management
specific_objective: RSO2.5. Promoting access to water and sustainable water management

project_name: EmpoweerNEET MED: Upskilling NEETs through Social and Solidarity Economy
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: جامعة النجاح الوطنية
topic_keywords: training | learning | education | workshop | skills | upskilling | capacity | guidelines | handbook | dissemination | communication | entrepreneur | entrepreneurs | entrepreneurship | startup | platform | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: EmpowerNEET is an innovative transnational initiative aimed at integrating NEETs—young people not engaged in employment, education, or training—into the Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) across the Mediterranean. These individuals face significant challenges, including high unemployment rates, economic exclusion, and limited access to quality education and vocational training, exacerbating social inequalities. EmpowerNEET leverages the underutilised potential of the SSE to promote inclusive economic growth and social integration.

By facilitating the transnational sharing of best practices and expertise, EmpowerNEET brings together entities from Palestine, Spain, Greece, Tunisia, Lebanon, and France to effectively address NEET marginalisation. The project offers comprehensive training programs to develop entrepreneurial and employment skills, provides practical experiences through job shadowing and mentoring, and ensures sustainability via supportive policy frameworks and ethical financing models.

EmpowerNEET aims to transition NEETs from economic and social marginalisation to empowerment. Key outputs include an extensive capacity-building program that equips 300 NEETs with essential theoretical and practical skills tailored for the SSE. Additionally, the project collaborates with local authorities to develop sustainable financing strategies, securing the long-term success of SSE initiatives and providing systemic support for NEETs.

The expected change includes improved employability and entrepreneurial capacity among NEETs. This project will benefit NEETs by enhancing their skills and opportunities, local governments by providing robust frameworks for NEETs and SSE support, and the broader community by stimulating economic growth and social cohesion. EmpowerNEET not only enhances the economic prospects of NEETs but also strengthens local communities by supporting SSEs, boosting economic vitality, and fostering inclusive growth.

~
SUMMARY: EmpowerNEET is an innovative transnational initiative aimed at integrating NEETs—young people not engaged in employment, education, or training—into the Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) across the Mediterranean. These individuals face significant challenges, including high unemployment rates, economic exclusion, and limited access to quality education and vocational training, exacerbating social inequalities. EmpowerNEET leverages the underutilised potential of the SSE to promote inclusive economic growth and social integration.

By facilitating the transnational sharing of best practices and expertise, EmpowerNEET brings together entities from Palestine, Spain, Greece, Tunisia, Lebanon, and France to effectively address NEET marginalisation. The project offers comprehensive training programs to develop entrepreneurial and employment skills, provides practical experiences through job shadowing and mentoring, and ensures sustainability via supportive policy frameworks and ethical financing models.

EmpowerNEET aims to transition NEETs from economic and social marginalisation to empowerment. Key outputs include an extensive capacity-building program that equips 300 NEETs with essential theoretical and practical skills tailored for the SSE. Additionally, the project collaborates with local authorities to develop sustainable financing strategies, securing the long-term success of SSE initiatives and providing systemic support for NEETs.

The expected change includes improved employability and entrepreneurial capacity among NEETs. This project will benefit NEETs by enhancing their skills and opportunities, local governments by providing robust frameworks for NEETs and SSE support, and the broader community by stimulating economic growth and social cohesion. EmpowerNEET not only enhances the economic prospects of NEETs but also strengthens local communities by supporting SSEs, boosting economic vitality, and fostering inclusive growth.
~

partner_name: University of Galway (Office of the Vice-President for Research & Innovation)
topic_keywords: knowledge transfer | pilot action | pilot actions | uptake | scalable | sme | smes
project_summary:
A pioneering initiative set to transform the cosmeceutical and nutraceutical industries by utilizing bioactives and biopolymers from microalgae. This project introduces innovative, low-impact methods to produce high-value ingredients, replacing synthetic additives while reducing water use and minimizing waste.
By optimizing microalgae cultivation and recovering valuable by-products, the project demonstrates scalable solutions that support a shift toward renewable, eco-friendly alternatives. Key outputs include pilot actions, innovative biotechnological solutions, and high-value bio-based products for industrial adoption, benefiting SMEs, researchers, and blue biotechnology industries.
With a transnational approach, the initiative integrates regional expertise, fosters knowledge transfer, and drives sustainable growth in the Atlantic’s blue economy. By accelerating innovation and competitiveness in the bioeconomy sector,it paves the way for a more sustainable future.

project_name: Transforming Rivers by Reducing Aquatic Plastic Pollution
partner_name: Associação do Instituto Superior Técnico para a Investigação e Desenvolvimento (Civil Engineering Research and Innovation for Sustainability)
project_summary:
TRAP addresses the critical transnational challenge of aquatic plastic pollution through innovative, eco-friendly solutions to reduce plastic litter reaching the Atlantic Ocean. The main goal is to produce guidelines to design hotspots to trap and retain plastics in rivers and estuaries. It offers an innovative approach to tackle aquatic plastic pollution by combining advanced digital tools, nature-based solutions and community engagement.
TRAP brings positive change in the efficiency of plastic removal strategies and better-informed management practices.
Key outputs include a digital management tool for identifying plastic retention hotspots, field-tested retention solutions, and practical guidelines for decision-makers. Beneficiaries range from environmental agencies to local authorities and riverine communities.
The project leverages transnational cooperation to ensure knowledge sharing, creating scalable, field-tested solutions that can be adapted across the Atlantic Area.
specific_objective: RSO2.7. Enhancing protection and preservation of nature, biodiversity and green infrastructure, including in urban areas, and reducing all forms of pollution

project_name: Mediterranean initiative to REgenerate wastewater Sludges for Optimal Use towaRd a Circular Economy
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: محافظة اريحا والأغوار
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | toolkit | guidelines | communication | reuse | beneficiaries | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

066. Waste water collection and treatment compliant with energy efficiency criteria
~
SUMMARY: MedRESOURCE tackles the pressing transnational challenges of water scarcity, land degradation, and waste management in the Mediterranean region. Currently, despite existing standards, WW sludge is still inadequately managed, leading to environmental harm and missed opportunities for resource recovery. MedRESOURCE will develop and demonstrate four innovative solutions to reuse wastewater sludge to enhance soil fertility, reduce environmental pollution, and foster sustainable agricultural practices.
The solutions will be jointly developed by a partnership of 6 organizations from 4 countries: Palestine, Italy, Tunisia and Jordan, supported by institutional and local associated partners. In each country a Living Lab will be set up to further develop existing solutions and to demonstrate their value to local communities, farmers, authorities and policy makers.
The expected change from MedRESOURCE is profound: converting waste into valuable resources, the project will significantly improve soil quality, reduce the need for chemical fertilizers, and mitigate pollution. This will enhance agricultural productivity and resilience to climate change, benefiting local farmers, communities, and the environment.
Key outputs are:
-	4 Living Labs (LL), each demonstrating a specific reuse of WW sludge in agriculture
-	A capacity building programme carried out in each LL, reaching about 200 beneficiaries
-	4 international study visist to the LL, promoting replicability and best practice exchange and reaching from 60 to 120 beneficiaries
-	Guidelines for policies development, providing references and standards at EU and MED level to integrate and better adapt regulatory frameworks to the innovative potential of WW sludge reuse.
MedRESOURCE offers a compelling solution to critical environmental issues, providing tangible benefits and fostering sustainable development across the Mediterranean. Supporting this project means investing in a greener, more resilient future for the region.
~

project_summary:
A pioneering initiative set to transform the cosmeceutical and nutraceutical industries by utilizing bioactives and biopolymers from microalgae. This project introduces innovative, low-impact methods to produce high-value ingredients, replacing synthetic additives while reducing water use and minimizing waste.
By optimizing microalgae cultivation and recovering valuable by-products, the project demonstrates scalable solutions that support a shift toward renewable, eco-friendly alternatives. Key outputs include pilot actions, innovative biotechnological solutions, and high-value bio-based products for industrial adoption, benefiting SMEs, researchers, and blue biotechnology industries.
With a transnational approach, the initiative integrates regional expertise, fosters knowledge transfer, and drives sustainable growth in the Atlantic’s blue economy. By accelerating innovation and competitiveness in the bioeconomy sector,it paves the way for a more sustainable future.

project_name: Cultivating Green Innovators: A Dynamic Approach to Smart, Sustainable, and Socially Inclusive Education for Mediterranean Youth
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: Πανεπιστήμιο Μακεδονίας
topic_keywords: training | learning | education | course | curriculum | workshop | skills | capacity building | capacity | methodologies | toolkit | guidelines | awareness | communication | stakeholder engagement | students | youth | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 2 - Youth-oriented projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: MEDSHIFTGREEN directly addresses the pressing transnational challenges of environmental degradation and resource depletion in the Mediterranean by revolutionizing education through the integration of nature-based solutions and green technologies into traditional curricula. This initiative seeks to transform the educational landscape, fostering a generation of youth who are well-equipped to tackle and mitigate ecological issues across borders. MEDSHIFTGREEN approach shifts from conventional learning paradigms to a holistic, sustainability-focused education model that provides young people with the critical skills necessary to innovate within the green economy. The expected change is profound as we aim to fill the current gap in specialized training in sustainable practices and increase environmental awareness among regional youth. Key outputs of the project include the establishment of Interactive Green Labs across Greek, Lebanese, Turkish & Cyprus educational institutions and beyond, integration of sustainability into academic programs via the NBS Curriculum, and the implementation of Green Skills Bootcamps that offer practical, economy-driven environmental education. These elements are designed to empower students with new competencies, provide educators with necessary resources and methodologies for teaching sustainability, and ultimately benefit local communities and industries through innovative, applicable environmental solutions developed by students. MEDSHIFTGREEN not only promises significant educational evolution but also ensures that Mediterranean youth are prepared to lead sustainable & inclusive growth in their communities, turning ecological challenges into opportunities for innovation and collaboration. This project will cultivate a generation that thrives on solving problems together, enhancing both their employability and their proactive engagement in preserving our planet.
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Green technologies, Innovation capacity and awareness-raising, Sustainable management of natural resources
specific_objective: RSO4.2. Improving equal access to inclusive and quality services in education, training and lifelong learning through developing accessible infrastructure, including by fostering resilience for distance and on-line education and training

project_name: Developing Inclusive Education in the Mediterranean
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: جامعة النجاح الوطنية
topic_keywords: training | trainings | learning | education | course | skills | capacity building | capacity development | capacity | knowledge transfer | toolkit | dissemination | communication | replication | scalability | beneficiaries | youth | women | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean is united by some major challenges related to the development of its different areas and communities, especially regarding the gap between cultural heritage developmental potential and the social and economic precariousness prevailing around important cultural sites.
Education, continuous training and skills development represent a key tool both for strengthening the potential of territories and for promoting inclusion, empowerment and social cohesion.
Furthermore, Cultural Heritage (CH) is one of the most sound, common and recognizable Mediterranean traits as well as an acknowledged driver for its development.
Accordingly, its main objective will be reinforcing and innovating the consortium's different learning/teaching systems to make them more integrated and compatible. Besides, the focus on Cultural Heritage will allow DIEM to exploit the acknowledged multiplier effect of CH-oriented activities in terms of know-how exchanging, empowerment of people and, eventually, economic growth.
Hence, DIEM is expected to contribute to social inclusion and to skills development for the unintegrated groups (youth, women, rural inhabitants and NEETs). Capitalizing on the experience of its partners, DIEM will move along 3 concurrent lines of development: deliver a cascade model of teaching/training programs for 3 groups (A-Leaders, B-Future Managers, C-Local communities); setup a toolkit for teaching/learning capable to combine knowledge, emerging technologies (i.e. AI) and the lessons learned from the pandemic; assume DIEM as an occasion for the first beneficiaries and for broader communities, the assets and territories belonging to the 6 countries of DIEM consortium (IT, EGY, JOR, TUN, ES,PS).
The project entails 3 technical WPs: Standardization (Partners design systems for international cooperation), Training around pilot areas (TOT courses and inclusion-oriented trainings), Scalability (replication on youth and extension to broader communities).
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Cultural heritage and arts, Education and training, Social inclusion and equal opportunities

project_name: Developing Inclusive Education in the Mediterranean
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: مركز ترميم للتدريب على المحافظة على التراث الثقافي والطبيعي
topic_keywords: training | trainings | learning | education | course | skills | capacity building | capacity development | capacity | knowledge transfer | toolkit | dissemination | communication | replication | scalability | beneficiaries | youth | women | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean is united by some major challenges related to the development of its different areas and communities, especially regarding the gap between cultural heritage developmental potential and the social and economic precariousness prevailing around important cultural sites.
Education, continuous training and skills development represent a key tool both for strengthening the potential of territories and for promoting inclusion, empowerment and social cohesion.
Furthermore, Cultural Heritage (CH) is one of the most sound, common and recognizable Mediterranean traits as well as an acknowledged driver for its development.
Accordingly, its main objective will be reinforcing and innovating the consortium's different learning/teaching systems to make them more integrated and compatible. Besides, the focus on Cultural Heritage will allow DIEM to exploit the acknowledged multiplier effect of CH-oriented activities in terms of know-how exchanging, empowerment of people and, eventually, economic growth.
Hence, DIEM is expected to contribute to social inclusion and to skills development for the unintegrated groups (youth, women, rural inhabitants and NEETs). Capitalizing on the experience of its partners, DIEM will move along 3 concurrent lines of development: deliver a cascade model of teaching/training programs for 3 groups (A-Leaders, B-Future Managers, C-Local communities); setup a toolkit for teaching/learning capable to combine knowledge, emerging technologies (i.e. AI) and the lessons learned from the pandemic; assume DIEM as an occasion for the first beneficiaries and for broader communities, the assets and territories belonging to the 6 countries of DIEM consortium (IT, EGY, JOR, TUN, ES,PS).
The project entails 3 technical WPs: Standardization (Partners design systems for international cooperation), Training around pilot areas (TOT courses and inclusion-oriented trainings), Scalability (replication on youth and extension to broader communities).
~

project_name: Social Ecological Impact Assessment for the Maritime Sector in the Atlantic Area
partner_name: NORMANDIE LITTORAL (REGION NORMANDIE )
topic_keywords: methodology | methodologies | toolkit | knowledge exchange | uptake | sme | smes | platform
project_summary:
Impact4Mar aims to accelerate the transition of SMEs in the maritime sector of the Atlantic area by facilitating an innovative access to tools and methodologies for assessing Impact, Risks and Opportunities (IRO) in Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) dimensions.
Through transnational knowledge exchange, Impact4Mar proposes an integrated approach based on a common methodology across the regional maritime ecosystems involved to: - Conduct an analysis of sustainability IROs assessment practices in the maritime sector - Prototype and validate a Toolkit through pilots in maritime SMEs -Capitalize and disseminate the results through a digital platform and bootcamps.
By offering maritime SME-focused solutions, the project empowers businesses to comply with EU sustainability regulations, align with the EU Green Deal and UN 2030 Agenda and enhance resilience, competitiveness and innovation as a catalyst for the Blue Economy’s sustainable development in Europe.
specific_objective: RSO1.1. Developing and enhancing research and innovation capacities and the uptake of advanced technologies

project_name: Mediterranean initiative to REgenerate wastewater Sludges for Optimal Use towaRd a Circular Economy
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: مهندسي الصرف الصحي الفلسطينية - Palestinian Wastewater Engineers Group (PWEG)
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | toolkit | guidelines | communication | reuse | beneficiaries | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

066. Waste water collection and treatment compliant with energy efficiency criteria
~
SUMMARY: MedRESOURCE tackles the pressing transnational challenges of water scarcity, land degradation, and waste management in the Mediterranean region. Currently, despite existing standards, WW sludge is still inadequately managed, leading to environmental harm and missed opportunities for resource recovery. MedRESOURCE will develop and demonstrate four innovative solutions to reuse wastewater sludge to enhance soil fertility, reduce environmental pollution, and foster sustainable agricultural practices.
The solutions will be jointly developed by a partnership of 6 organizations from 4 countries: Palestine, Italy, Tunisia and Jordan, supported by institutional and local associated partners. In each country a Living Lab will be set up to further develop existing solutions and to demonstrate their value to local communities, farmers, authorities and policy makers.
The expected change from MedRESOURCE is profound: converting waste into valuable resources, the project will significantly improve soil quality, reduce the need for chemical fertilizers, and mitigate pollution. This will enhance agricultural productivity and resilience to climate change, benefiting local farmers, communities, and the environment.
Key outputs are:
-	4 Living Labs (LL), each demonstrating a specific reuse of WW sludge in agriculture
-	A capacity building programme carried out in each LL, reaching about 200 beneficiaries
-	4 international study visist to the LL, promoting replicability and best practice exchange and reaching from 60 to 120 beneficiaries
-	Guidelines for policies development, providing references and standards at EU and MED level to integrate and better adapt regulatory frameworks to the innovative potential of WW sludge reuse.
MedRESOURCE offers a compelling solution to critical environmental issues, providing tangible benefits and fostering sustainable development across the Mediterranean. Supporting this project means investing in a greener, more resilient future for the region.
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Soil and air quality, Sustainable management of natural resources, Water management
specific_objective: RSO2.5. Promoting access to water and sustainable water management

project_name: Transforming Rivers by Reducing Aquatic Plastic Pollution
partner_name: Atlantic Technological University (Research Office)
project_summary:
TRAP addresses the critical transnational challenge of aquatic plastic pollution through innovative, eco-friendly solutions to reduce plastic litter reaching the Atlantic Ocean. The main goal is to produce guidelines to design hotspots to trap and retain plastics in rivers and estuaries. It offers an innovative approach to tackle aquatic plastic pollution by combining advanced digital tools, nature-based solutions and community engagement.
TRAP brings positive change in the efficiency of plastic removal strategies and better-informed management practices.
Key outputs include a digital management tool for identifying plastic retention hotspots, field-tested retention solutions, and practical guidelines for decision-makers. Beneficiaries range from environmental agencies to local authorities and riverine communities.
The project leverages transnational cooperation to ensure knowledge sharing, creating scalable, field-tested solutions that can be adapted across the Atlantic Area.
specific_objective: RSO2.7. Enhancing protection and preservation of nature, biodiversity and green infrastructure, including in urban areas, and reducing all forms of pollution

project_name: Sustainable Microalgae and Cyanobacteria-Based Bioactive Compounds for Cosmeceuticals and Nutraceuticals
partner_name: COSMETIC VALLEY (Europe & International)
topic_keywords: knowledge transfer | pilot action | pilot actions | uptake | scalable | sme | smes
project_summary:
A pioneering initiative set to transform the cosmeceutical and nutraceutical industries by utilizing bioactives and biopolymers from microalgae. This project introduces innovative, low-impact methods to produce high-value ingredients, replacing synthetic additives while reducing water use and minimizing waste.
By optimizing microalgae cultivation and recovering valuable by-products, the project demonstrates scalable solutions that support a shift toward renewable, eco-friendly alternatives. Key outputs include pilot actions, innovative biotechnological solutions, and high-value bio-based products for industrial adoption, benefiting SMEs, researchers, and blue biotechnology industries.
With a transnational approach, the initiative integrates regional expertise, fosters knowledge transfer, and drives sustainable growth in the Atlantic’s blue economy. By accelerating innovation and competitiveness in the bioeconomy sector,it paves the way for a more sustainable future.
specific_objective: RSO1.1. Developing and enhancing research and innovation capacities and the uptake of advanced technologies

project_name: Optimizing the Algal Biomass Value Chains for Sustainable Economic Growth in the Atlantic Area
partner_name: ASOCIACIÓN BIOGA – CLUSTER TECNOLÓXICO EMPRESARIAL DE CIENCIAS DA VIDA (Internationalisation)
project_summary:
The project focuses on achieving an efficient and sustainable utilization of micro and macroalgae in the Atlantic Area by organizing and optimizing the entire algal biomass value chains.
It involves stakeholders mapping and cooperation, challenges analysis, models development powered with intelligent digital tools, and the generation of market-oriented prototypes for the cosmetics and food sectors, while fostering collaboration across Spain, Portugal, France and Ireland.
The approach of the project integrates sustainable practices, innovative technologies, and stakeholder insights to optimize harvesting, valorisation, production and consumer involvement. project_name: Boosting the environmental traceability of the Atlantic seafood sector: sea to fork transparency and consumer engagement
partner_name: BIM Ireland's Seafood Development Agency (Development & Innovation Unit)
project_summary:
SMART4SEA introduces an innovative environmental traceability system to enhance transparency and consumer trust in the Atlantic seafood sector. This initiative addresses major issues such as seafood fraud, ecological damage, marine debris, and resource inefficiency.
By integrating advanced digital tools, circular economy strategies, and collaborative efforts, SMART4SEA seeks to enhance sustainability and transparency throughout the seafood supply chain.
The project will deliver a comprehensive tracking system, tools for real-time data gathering, platforms for engaging consumers, and pilot projects to showcase its effectiveness. Beneficiaries include fishermen, processors, consumers, and authorities, who will all gain access to innovative tools and reliable information to support sustainable practices.
Transnational cooperation is crucial, as it brings together diverse expertise to develop effective solutions and ensure the project's scalability across the Atlantic Area and beyond.
specific_objective: RSO2.6. Promoting the transition to a circular and resource efficient economy
suggested_angle: Package digital skills and transformation outputs into structured beneficiary learning tracks.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

project_name: Transforming Rivers by Reducing Aquatic Plastic Pollution
partner_name: COOPERATIVE D'ACTIVITE ET D'EMPLOI DANS LES METIERS DU PATRIMOINE (CDE PETRA PATRIMONIA) (Petra Maritima)
project_summary:
TRAP addresses the critical transnational challenge of aquatic plastic pollution through innovative, eco-friendly solutions to reduce plastic litter reaching the Atlantic Ocean. The main goal is to produce guidelines to design hotspots to trap and retain plastics in rivers and estuaries. It offers an innovative approach to tackle aquatic plastic pollution by combining advanced digital tools, nature-based solutions and community engagement.
TRAP brings positive change in the efficiency of plastic removal strategies and better-informed management practices.
Key outputs include a digital management tool for identifying plastic retention hotspots, field-tested retention solutions, and practical guidelines for decision-makers. Beneficiaries range from environmental agencies to local authorities and riverine communities.
The project leverages transnational cooperation to ensure knowledge sharing, creating scalable, field-tested solutions that can be adapted across the Atlantic Area.
specific_objective: RSO2.7. Enhancing protection and preservation of nature, biodiversity and green infrastructure, including in urban areas, and reducing all forms of pollution
suggested_angle: Move from dissemination assets to reusable knowledge-transfer products and measurable training.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

project_name: Restoration of Damaged Ecosystems and Valorisation of Invasive Algae and Salt-Tolerant Plants
partner_name: Universidade de Santiago de Compostela (Faculty of Veterinary Science - Department of Pharmacology, Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Technology )
project_summary:
REVALGAE challenges the multilevel problems caused by the spread of invasive algae and salt-tolerant plants in the Atlantic Area. To transform this problem into an opportunity, the partnership will develop harvesting procedures and biomass valorisation routes that preserve biodiversity and marine ecosystems and foster economic growth in Atlantic regions.
The REVALGAE Pilots will identify areas requiring urgent action, and scalable and replicable biomass harvesting and ecosystem restoration procedures will be tested. The collected biomass will be valorised to obtain new bioactive compounds, helping to turn the invasive species problem into new opportunities to strengthen the Atlantic Area’s value chains. partner_name: BIM Ireland's Seafood Development Agency (Development & Innovation Unit)
project_summary:
The project focuses on achieving an efficient and sustainable utilization of micro and macroalgae in the Atlantic Area by organizing and optimizing the entire algal biomass value chains.
It involves stakeholders mapping and cooperation, challenges analysis, models development powered with intelligent digital tools, and the generation of market-oriented prototypes for the cosmetics and food sectors, while fostering collaboration across Spain, Portugal, France and Ireland.
The approach of the project integrates sustainable practices, innovative technologies, and stakeholder insights to optimize harvesting, valorisation, production and consumer involvement. By aligning industry needs with environmental and market demands, the project promotes a sustainable and economic growth in the algae-based biomass industry.

project_name: INSTITUTIONAL COOPERATION TO ENHANCE THE EFFECTIVENESS AND EFFICIENCY OF PUBLIC HEALTH POLICY LINKED TO THE INTRODUCTION OF TROPICAL DISEASES IN MACARONESIA
programme: 2014 - 2020 INTERREG V-A Spain - Portugal (Madeira - Açores - Canarias (MAC))
partner_name: Dirección General de Salúd Pública del Servicio
project_summary: AEDENET is a project of collaboration and cooperation between administrations and public health support institutes of the Canary Islands and Cape Verde with the aim of improving the effectiveness and results of this public health policy vis-à-vis its citizens. For this, joint collaboration and coordination agreements are established, protocols of action tested in the territory, both Canarian and Cape Verdean are established, training is carried out among professionals and tools and actions of dissemination and awareness are carried out between them and the general population. All within the framework of the fight against the introduction and spread of vector-borne tropical diseases (mainly mosquitoes). Finally, it is expected to achieve a safer and stronger Macaronesian region in the face of this threat that will undoubtedly be greater in the coming years with climate change and the globalisation of transports and movements of people and goods.
suggested_angle: Turn training, workshops and capacity-building assets into scalable learning paths.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

project_name: Re-use of marine sediments and circular economy
partner_name: University College Cork (MaREI Research Centre)
project_summary:
The project aims to foster innovative approaches for the beneficial reuse of marine sediments, supporting circular economy strategies in the Atlantic Area. The overall objective is to boost synergies to pave the way for re-use alternatives for local destinations, and demonstrate that marine sediments are a valuable resource with significant potential.
By combining scientific research with innovation, the project seeks to address the transnational challenge of shifting the perception of these sediments from waste to a reusable resource, addressing environmental, regulatory and economic common challenges. Through cross-border and transdisciplinary collaboration and stakeholder engagement, the project will encourage, by demonstrating potential applications, the identification of new value chains that promote reducing waste, and enhance circular loops for the sustainable reuse of marine sediments among the maritime and port communities of the Atlantic Area.
specific_objective: RSO2.6. Promoting the transition to a circular and resource efficient economy

project_name: Boosting the environmental traceability of the Atlantic seafood sector: sea to fork transparency and consumer engagement
partner_name: Valorial (Valorial Europe)
project_summary:
SMART4SEA introduces an innovative environmental traceability system to enhance transparency and consumer trust in the Atlantic seafood sector. This initiative addresses major issues such as seafood fraud, ecological damage, marine debris, and resource inefficiency.
By integrating advanced digital tools, circular economy strategies, and collaborative efforts, SMART4SEA seeks to enhance sustainability and transparency throughout the seafood supply chain.
The project will deliver a comprehensive tracking system, tools for real-time data gathering, platforms for engaging consumers, and pilot projects to showcase its effectiveness. Beneficiaries include fishermen, processors, consumers, and authorities, who will all gain access to innovative tools and reliable information to support sustainable practices.
Transnational cooperation is crucial, as it brings together diverse expertise to develop effective solutions and ensure the project's scalability across the Atlantic Area and beyond.
specific_objective: RSO2.6. Promoting the transition to a circular and resource efficient economy
suggested_angle: Package digital skills and transformation outputs into structured beneficiary learning tracks.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

project_name: Routes of the Great Atlantic Explorations
partner_name: Atlantic Cities (Atlantic Cities)
project_summary:
Setting up the Atlantic network for the valorization of the great nautical expeditions is the overall project objective. This is totally innovative to date. This objective represents the transnational challenge jointly tackled by the partnership.
Added value brought by the figure of the Council of Europe Cultural Route will be applied to the valorization of the Great Atlantic Expeditions legacy. Transnational strategies and action plans will drive 6 project activities embedded in 3 work packages. Activities address the set-up of a scientific committee, of an Atlantic Association governance body, of a new Council of Europe Cultural Route, of the implementation of 4 Pilot actions and of enhancing visibility and innovation to the new Cultural Route. All of this could only be achievable on the basis of transnational cooperation of the project partnership and other territorial partners not involved in the project. The durability of the project outputs is ensured.
specific_objective: RSO4.6. Enhancing the role of culture and sustainable tourism in economic development, social inclusion and social innovation
suggested_angle: Structure project methods, outputs and pilot lessons into reusable training for target beneficiaries.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

partner_name: Universidade NOVA de Lisboa - Faculdade de Ciencias e Tecnologia (Department of Chemistry - LAQV REQUIMTE)
project_summary:
REVALGAE challenges the multilevel problems caused by the spread of invasive algae and salt-tolerant plants in the Atlantic Area. To transform this problem into an opportunity, the partnership will develop harvesting procedures and biomass valorisation routes that preserve biodiversity and marine ecosystems and foster economic growth in Atlantic regions.
The REVALGAE Pilots will identify areas requiring urgent action, and scalable and replicable biomass harvesting and ecosystem restoration procedures will be tested. The collected biomass will be valorised to obtain new bioactive compounds, helping to turn the invasive species problem into new opportunities to strengthen the Atlantic Area’s value chains. To achieve this, REVALGAE brings together a multidisciplinary team, working in collaboration with Quadruple Helix actors, that combines expertise in environmental preservation, chemical compound development, industry, and policy engagement to transform invasive species into high-value resources.
specific_objective: RSO2.7. Enhancing protection and preservation of nature, biodiversity and green infrastructure, including in urban areas, and reducing all forms of pollution
suggested_angle: Move from dissemination assets to reusable knowledge-transfer products and measurable training.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

project_name: Re-use of marine sediments and circular economy
partner_name: The Port of Cork Company (Engineering)
project_summary:
The project aims to foster innovative approaches for the beneficial reuse of marine sediments, supporting circular economy strategies in the Atlantic Area. The overall objective is to boost synergies to pave the way for re-use alternatives for local destinations, and demonstrate that marine sediments are a valuable resource with significant potential.
By combining scientific research with innovation, the project seeks to address the transnational challenge of shifting the perception of these sediments from waste to a reusable resource, addressing environmental, regulatory and economic common challenges. Through cross-border and transdisciplinary collaboration and stakeholder engagement, the project will encourage, by demonstrating potential applications, the identification of new value chains that promote reducing waste, and enhance circular loops for the sustainable reuse of marine sediments among the maritime and port communities of the Atlantic Area.
specific_objective: RSO2.6. Promoting the transition to a circular and resource efficient economy
suggested_angle: Move from dissemination assets to reusable knowledge-transfer products and measurable training.

project_name: Optimizing the Algal Biomass Value Chains for Sustainable Economic Growth in the Atlantic Area
partner_name: Kerry County Council (Economic Development and Tourism)
project_summary:
The project focuses on achieving an efficient and sustainable utilization of micro and macroalgae in the Atlantic Area by organizing and optimizing the entire algal biomass value chains.
It involves stakeholders mapping and cooperation, challenges analysis, models development powered with intelligent digital tools, and the generation of market-oriented prototypes for the cosmetics and food sectors, while fostering collaboration across Spain, Portugal, France and Ireland.
The approach of the project integrates sustainable practices, innovative technologies, and stakeholder insights to optimize harvesting, valorisation, production and consumer involvement. By aligning industry needs with environmental and market demands, the project promotes a sustainable and economic growth in the algae-based biomass industry.
specific_objective: RSO1.1. Developing and enhancing research and innovation capacities and the uptake of advanced technologies
suggested_angle: Convert project outputs into reusable SME and entrepreneurship training journeys.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

project_name: STRENGTHENING THE LINKS BETWEEN MACARONESICAS CITIES AND THEIR NEIGHBOURHOOD IN TERMS OF GOOD GOVERNANCE AND DEVELOPMENT
programme: 2014 - 2020 INTERREG V-A Spain - Portugal (Madeira - Açores - Canarias (MAC))
partner_name: AYUNTAMIENTO DE LAS PALMAS DE GRAN
project_summary:
The LAZOS project seeks to contribute to the development of the capital cities of the countries of Cape Verde, Mauritania, Senegal and Las Palmas de Gran Canaria and to promote more efficient and effective models of municipal organisation and management, through a participatory process of exchange of experience, information, training, participatory diagnosis and improvement plans. Above all, it is intended to address the problem of the growth of African cities (it is estimated that they double their population in 20 years). All this will help to improve the management capacities of cities, especially in urban and environmental areas and strengthen cooperation ties at political and technical level, as set out in the Partnership Protocol signed by the partners in July

2017. This project is the beginning of a permanent work of the partner cities, which will serve as the basis for implementing new projects and programs in common areas in the future.
suggested_angle: Turn training, workshops and capacity-building assets into scalable learning paths.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

project_name: ICT GOVERNANCE
programme: 2014 - 2020 INTERREG V-A Spain - Portugal (Madeira - Açores - Canarias (MAC))
partner_name: Agencia Canaria de Investigación, Innovación y Sociedad
project_summary:
The DYNAMIC-EGOV project aims to promote the use of ICT, related to e-government in the governments of Macaronesia, improving public services, productivity and efficiency of public personnel, as well as information and formalities and services with citizens. To achieve this objective, the project partners will mainly develop five axes of action:

(1) Coordination with all the actions being carried out to improve governance, promoting a transnational cooperation network for the use of ICT.

(2) Develop a unique inventory of existing ICT applications, design a strategy for leveraging them in each government.

(3) Develop and use ICT tools, making real cases.

(4) Coordination with the ICT sector of each region, for the evolution, maintenance and growth of ICT eGovernment tools.

(5) Dissemination and dissemination of the advantages of these tools.

project_name: MEDICAL SCIENCE FRUGAL INNOVATION CENTER OF MACARONESIA
programme: 2014 - 2020 INTERREG V-A Spain - Portugal (Madeira - Açores - Canarias (MAC))
partner_name: Servicio Canario de la Salud
project_summary:
The CIFCAM Project aims to democratise access to advanced medical technology by introducing frugal innovation processes, a concept that includes those production processes that allow the obtaining of products of equal utility to other pre-existing ones, but with a complexity and cost of production significantly lower. The innovative advances of these characteristics within the health field allow the implementation of medical techniques or processes that lead to a reduction in the costs of existing technologies, which facilitates the access of state-of-the-art medical technologies to strata of the population that currently does not have access to them.
The project will have from the beginning with the participation of researchers specialised in the field. These will train health professionals from the Canary Islands, Cape Verde and Senegal in the most advanced techniques of developing low-cost, easy-to-implement medical devices.
suggested_angle: Structure project methods, outputs and pilot lessons into reusable training for target beneficiaries.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.
